title: thalictrum minus subsp. jacquinianum
intro: Thalictrum minus subsp. jacquinianum, also known as Meadow Rue, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Thalictrum minus subsp. jacquinianum include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as aiding in digestion and reducing blood pressure. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in water or using them as a tincture, and it is often used in teas, infusions, and salves.
